title: Date types
description: Quarter, reporting period, date range, and UTC date-time types. All date and timestamp fields across the data model reference these definitions.

Date.description: ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD). Used for business dates such as trade dates, settlement dates, close dates, and due dates. Time-zone-agnostic — represents a calendar day, not a point in time. Example: '2024-06-15'

UtcDateTime.description: ISO 8601 date-time in UTC. MUST end with 'Z' (Zulu time) — offsets like '+05:30' are rejected. Used for system timestamps (created_at, updated_at, settled_at) where a precise point in time is needed. Consumers should convert to local time for display. Example: '2024-06-15T14:30:00Z'

Quarter.description: A fiscal quarter. Used to identify reporting periods, fund vintages, and performance windows. Example: {"year": 2024, "quarter": 2} represents Q2 2024 (April–June for calendar-year filers).

year.description: Four-digit calendar year. Range limited to 1900–2100 for data integrity. Example: 2024

quarter.description: Quarter number (1–4). Q1 = Jan–Mar, Q2 = Apr–Jun, Q3 = Jul–Sep, Q4 = Oct–Dec for calendar-year filers. For non-calendar fiscal years, quarter boundaries follow the entity's fiscal year-end. Example: 2

ReportingPeriod.description: A reporting period defined by explicit start and end dates, with an optional quarter reference. Used for NAV statements, financial reports, and performance calculations where the exact date boundaries matter. Example: {"start_date": "2024-04-01", "end_date": "2024-06-30", "quarter": {"year": 2024, "quarter": 2}}

start_date.description: First day of the reporting period (inclusive). Example: '2024-04-01'

end_date.description: Last day of the reporting period (inclusive). Must be on or after start_date. Example: '2024-06-30'

quarter.description: The fiscal quarter this period corresponds to, if applicable. Omit for non-quarterly periods (e.g., annual reports, stub periods).

DateRange.description: An arbitrary date range for filtering, queries, or specifying validity windows. Unlike ReportingPeriod, this has no fiscal/reporting semantics — it is purely a start/end date pair. Example: {"start_date": "2024-01-01", "end_date": "2024-12-31"}

end_date.description: End of the range (inclusive). Must be on or after start_date. Example: '2024-12-31'
